data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Shared Interactive Agent Session Runtime

## Problem Statement

Terminal-manager hard-codes `dialect === "claude"` as the sole interactive agent (see `terminal-manager-initial-child.ts:90` and `terminal-manager-take-child.ts:62`). All other dialects (codex, copilot, opencode) are forced into one-shot prompt mode with `stdio: ["ignore", ...]`. Additionally, I/O wiring logic (stdin helpers, auto-answer AskUserQuestion, stdout parsing, close/error handlers) is duplicated nearly verbatim between `terminal-manager-initial-io.ts` and `terminal-manager-take-child.ts`.

## Architecture Overview

### New Abstraction: `AgentSessionCapabilities`

Add a capability declaration interface to `agent-adapter.ts` so each dialect declares what it supports, replacing boolean `isInteractive` checks with structured capability lookups.

```typescript
export interface AgentSessionCapabilities {
  interactive: boolean;
  promptTransport: "stdin" | "args";
  followUpSupport: boolean;
  stdinDrain: "grace-close" | "immediate" | "none";
  watchdog: { timeoutMs: number } | null;
}
```

Add `resolveCapabilities(dialect: AgentDialect): AgentSessionCapabilities`.

### New Module: `agent-session-io.ts` — Shared I/O Runtime

Extract the duplicated I/O logic from `terminal-manager-initial-io.ts` and `terminal-manager-take-child.ts` into a single shared module. Centralizes: stdin management, auto-answer AskUserQuestion, stdout wiring (JSONL parsing, normalization, result detection), stderr wiring, close/error handlers, and process-group kill escalation.

### New Module: `agent-session-watchdog.ts` — Inactivity Watchdog

Centralized watchdog: starts on session creation, resets on stdout activity, fires configurable timeout per dialect capability, emits warning then kills process group.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add `AgentSessionCapabilities` to `agent-adapter.ts` (~55 lines added)
- Add interface + `resolveCapabilities()` function
- Claude: interactive=true, promptTransport=stdin, followUpSupport=true, stdinDrain=grace-close, watchdog=null
- Codex/Copilot/OpenCode: interactive=false, promptTransport=args, followUpSupport=false, stdinDrain=none, watchdog={timeoutMs:300000}

### Step 2: Add `buildInteractiveArgs()` to `agent-adapter.ts` (~20 lines)
- Move interactive args from initial-child:232-239 and take-child:132-139 into agent-adapter
- Extension point for future interactive dialects

### Step 3: Create `agent-session-io.ts` — shared I/O runtime (~250 lines new, ~300 lines removed)
- `SessionIoContext` interface
- `IoState` (unified from InitialChildState + ChildIoState)
- `createIoState(caps, autoShipPrompt?)`
- Deduplicated: closeInput, cancelInputClose, scheduleInputClose, sendUserTurn, autoAnswerAskUser, wireChildIo, flushLineBuffer

### Step 4: Create `agent-session-watchdog.ts` (~60 lines)
- Watchdog class: constructor(timeoutMs, onTimeout), reset(), stop()
- Used by wireChildIo() when caps.watchdog is non-null

### Step 5: Refactor `terminal-manager-initial-child.ts` (~80 lines removed, ~20 added)
- Replace `dialect === "claude"` with `resolveCapabilities(dialect)`
- Replace inline I/O wiring with `wireChildIo()` from agent-session-io
- Keep session lifecycle in this file

### Step 6: Refactor `terminal-manager-take-child.ts` (~200 lines removed, ~30 added)
- Same pattern: capability-driven logic, shared I/O runtime
- Keep take-loop-specific iteration/beat-state logging

### Step 7: Slim `terminal-manager-initial-io.ts` (shrinks from ~512 to ~120 lines)
- Remove functions centralized in agent-session-io
- Keep InitialChildState creation and resultFollowUp() (unique to initial sessions)
- Re-export shared functions for backward compat during transition

### Step 8: Add conformance tests (~530 lines)
- `agent-session-capabilities.test.ts` (~150 lines): resolveCapabilities returns correct per-dialect values
- `agent-session-io.test.ts` (~300 lines): turn completion, session exit, timeout, retry, abort — parameterized across dialects
- `agent-session-watchdog.test.ts` (~80 lines): fire, reset, stop

### Step 9: Update existing tests (~9 files)
- Update imports to reference shared module
- Mock agent-session-io instead of individual I/O functions
- Ensure all existing tests pass

## Dependency Graph

Step 1 → Step 2 → Steps 5,6
Step 1 → Step 3 → Steps 5,6,7
Step 1 → Step 4 → Step 3
Step 8 after Steps 1-4; Step 9 after Steps 5-7

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|------------|
| Breaking existing test mocks | Medium | Run full test suite after each step |
| File length violations (500-line limit) | Low | agent-session-io.ts ~250 lines |
| Take-loop behavioral regression | Medium | Existing tests cover retry, rollback, invariant enforcement |
| Circular imports | Low | New modules only depend downward on agent-adapter, terminal-manager-format |

## Complexity Estimate

- New code: ~600 lines (runtime + watchdog + capabilities)
- Removed code: ~580 lines (deduplicated I/O)
- New test code: ~530 lines
- Net: ~+550 lines (mostly tests)
- Files: 5 modified + 5 new
- Effort: Medium-large

## Acceptance Criteria Mapping

| Criterion | Steps |
|-----------|-------|
| 1. Shared interface for capabilities, events, lifecycle hooks | 1, 3, 4 |
| 2. Terminal-manager uses shared abstraction, not Claude-only branching | 5, 6, 7 |
| 3. Centralizes watchdog, process-group kill, close-input, follow-up | 3, 4 |
| 4. Conformance tests for turn completion, exit, timeout, retry, abort | 8, 9 |
